name: Ray Heindorf
description: American songwriter, composer, conductor, and arranger (1908-1980)
abstract: Raymond John Heindorf (August 25, 1908 – February 3, 1980) was an American composer and songwriter who was noted for his work in film.

about_quotes.speaker: Skitch Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: I lived with every day... I worked for — my first boss at MGM was a man by the name of Herbert Stothart... I saw a friend... who was kind of an assistant director for Warner Brothers... we started talking about Max Steiner and Ray Heindorf and all of these people that I lived with every day
about_quotes.doc_title: Skitch Henderson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Milford, Connecticut, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Trummy Young
about_quotes.evidence: we got that spot -- was Ray Heindorf ... he liked the band ... And we did quite a lot of ... business out there when we played this room. ... But we made the movie the same time we were out there.
